game.title: If I Was President
game.subtitle: Can you run Turkey?
game.disclaimer: This game is entirely fictional. Any resemblance to real events or persons is coincidental.

cards.c001_event: Inflation has exceeded 80%. Markets panic. Citizens cry looking at price tags in stores.
cards.c001_context: The Central Bank recommends an emergency interest rate hike.
cards.c001_yes_response: Markets calmed down but citizens can't get loans. Opposition shouts 'interest impoverishes!'
cards.c001_no_response: People are happy in the short term but the lira collapsed. IMF is at the door.
cards.c002_event: A Gulf country offers 50 billion dollars in investment. In return, a strategic port will be leased for 99 years.
cards.c002_yes_response: Money flowed in but opposition took to the streets shouting 'we sold our homeland!'
cards.c002_no_response: Nationalist circles applaud you. No investment but honor remains.
cards.c003_event: Minimum wage debate escalates. Worker unions demand 50% raise. Business world says 'we'll close'.
cards.c003_yes_response: Workers are dancing in the streets. But some factories closed.
cards.c003_no_response: Business world relaxed. But unions are heading to general strike.
cards.c004_event: Should crypto exchanges be taxed? Youth says 'don't touch', treasury is empty.
cards.c004_yes_response: Money entered treasury but crypto youth rebelled.
cards.c004_no_response: Youth loves you. But tax evasion continues.
cards.c005_event: Armed conflict erupted at the border. Our soldiers are under fire. Other side says 'accident'.
cards.c005_yes_response: Military is thrilled. But international pressure increased and dollar soared.
cards.c005_no_response: World praised you. But soldiers grumble 'we're tied up'.
cards.c006_event: Intelligence report: Major cyber attack preparation. To prevent it, all government systems must shut down for 48 hours.
cards.c006_no: Take the risk, keep it open
cards.c006_yes_response: Attack prevented but e-government didn't work for 2 days. Citizens went crazy.
cards.c006_no_response: No problem so far. But the general said 'responsibility is yours'.
cards.c007_event: NATO ally wants a major exercise. 10,000 foreign soldiers will be deployed on our territory.
cards.c007_yes_response: West is happy. But nationalists shout 'our territory is occupied!'
cards.c007_no_response: People applaud. But cold winds blow in NATO.
cards.c008_event: Opposition leader invites you to a live debate. Millions will watch.
cards.c008_yes_response: You won the debate! Polls jumped 5 points.
cards.c008_no_response: Opposition headlines 'he ran away'. Your reputation damaged.
cards.c009_event: A viral video targeting you on social media. Millions of views. Content is partially false.
cards.c009_yes_response: Video removed but 'censorship' news spread worldwide. Press freedom index dropped.
cards.c009_no_response: You acted transparently. People trusted you, even if some didn't believe.
cards.c010_event: Elections in 6 months. Coalition partner wants early elections or will topple the government.
cards.c010_yes_response: Election campaign started. Everything is uncertain.
cards.c010_no_response: You became a minority government. Every vote is a crisis.
cards.c011_event: University students in the streets due to housing crisis. Dorm fees tripled.
cards.c011_yes_response: Students applaud you. But dorm operators sued.
cards.c011_no_response: Scholarships increased but dorm problem unsolved. Protests continue.
cards.c012_event: Earthquake zone reconstruction is slow. Citizens living in containers for 2 years.
cards.c012_yes_response: Construction accelerated. But budget deficit grew.
cards.c012_no_response: Private sector moved slowly. People said 'we're abandoned'.
cards.c013_event: Health system crisis: Doctors leaving for Europe due to 'brain drain'. Hospitals understaffed.
cards.c013_yes_response: Doctors stayed. But health budget exploded.
cards.c014_event: Environmental disaster: Factory waste polluted the river. 500,000 people's drinking water at risk.
cards.c014_yes_response: Environmentalists hailed you as hero. 3,000 workers lost jobs.
cards.c014_no_response: Factory paid fine and continued. Environmental groups sued you.
cards.c015_event: Historic peace agreement possible with neighbor. 50 years of enmity could end.
cards.c015_yes_response: You're nominated for Nobel Peace Prize! But nationalists shout 'traitor!'
cards.c015_no_response: Tough stance appreciated. But opportunity lost.
cards.c016_event: UN Security Council vote: You must choose between West and East.
cards.c016_yes_response: EU membership talks reopened. But Russian and Chinese ambassadors protested.
cards.c016_no_response: Relations with East strengthened. But Western investment dropped.
cards.c017_event: Tourism season opens. Proposal to tax foreign tourists in foreign currency.
cards.c017_yes_response: Foreign currency entered treasury. But tourist numbers dropped 20%.
cards.c017_no_response: Tourism boomed! But you missed the opportunity.
cards.c018_event: Refugee crisis escalates. 2 million new refugees waiting at border. EU offers money but public opposes.
cards.c018_yes_response: EU funds arrived but domestic politics erupted.
cards.c018_no_response: People applaud. But international human rights groups condemned you.
cards.c019_event: New defense project: Domestic fighter jet requires 10 years, 30 billion dollars.
cards.c019_yes_response: Military thrilled. But budget constraints for 10 years ahead.
cards.c019_no_response: Quick solution but military sad 'we're dependent on others'.
cards.c020_event: Pressure to ban a famous artist's concert. Called 'immoral'.
cards.c020_yes_response: Conservative circles happy. But youth took to streets for 'freedom'.
cards.c020_no_response: Youth loves you. But conservative media targeted you.
